title: Safari Adds scrollend Event Support, Completing Baseline Browser Coverage
summary: Safari's release of version 26.2 in December introduced support for the scrollend event, completing its alignment with major browsers. This event signals when scrolling has definitively ended, enabling more reliable interactions without the need for workarounds. It improves performance for developers managing UI updates and data fetching based on scroll completion. By Daniel Curtis

title: Podcast: Tiger Teams, Evals and Agents: The New AI Engineering Playbook

By Sam Bhagwat
